preamble: The fortition of a rhotic trill or tap to a stop is a sound change where the vibrating or momentary contact is replaced by a full closure. This is often observed in intervocalic positions in specific language families.

examples.note: Intervocalic alveolar trill shifts to a stop in several Oceanic dialects.
Occasional fortition of rhotics in southern Italian dialects.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology.
